Lucas Premat.author: Lucas Premat
Lucas Premat.text: I know about them, but how do you integrate, can you give advice?

The discipline to do what you planned no matter what is PRIMARY. Then you need to program your environment to help you, not sabotage you. If a person doesn't know what fucks up their motivation, mood and willpower or what gives him strength, it's the first thing he needs to do. Know thyself. After that comes aligned action. Another big one is to understand that you only need to win the internal battle in the moment. To go through the lowest point, deepest darkness... and finish the day on top. It's not about perfection, almost nobody knows how to integrate these habits while living a normal life. I failed soooo many times... Key is to keep coming back to the path and not give up. . . so this is the core of integration. . . what habits would you like to integrate Lucas?

Lisa Bartolomucci.author: Dan Gibson
Lisa Bartolomucci.text: There is not a singer in the world that doesn't struggle with this. . . Even grammy-winning singers like JohnnySwim. Their breath capacity is gigantic. And still, in a live video, Abbie takes a huge gasp breath, panics, and almost misses a note. This is the highest level of singing. So how do they avoid messing up? They plan where to take a breath far in advance. . . Like 3 days before the performance, they decide we will breathe HERE, HERE, and HERE. And then with the band's music sheet, they draw lines: inhale, inhale, inhale. All the singers do it. They write INHALE right on the music timeline.

Naya Stone.title: honest question: how do you navigate the space between burnout and belief?
Naya Stone.body: 1d  • General discussionhonest question: how do you navigate the space between burnout and belief?I’m still committed to what i’m building, but i’m in a tight season mentally and financially.I'm not looking to quit nor am I really in a position to, just trying to understand how others have moved through this phase.Thoughts, personal stories, welcome. 🙏

Naya Stone.post_title: honest question: how do you navigate the space between burnout and belief?
Naya Stone.author: Dan Gibson
Naya Stone.text: The Tao reminds us: In spring the trees blossom but we can't force them to grow faster. Ideas and new approaches for business are exactly the same. I think it's also very important that the quality of our daily life is not determined by how well or not well the business is doing. . .

Naya Stone.post_title: honest question: how do you navigate the space between burnout and belief?
Naya Stone.author: Naya Stone
Naya Stone.text: @Daniel Carter That line about not tying the quality of our daily life to business performance is a great reminder. Thank you for sharing that. 🙏

Naya Stone.post_title: honest question: how do you navigate the space between burnout and belief?
Naya Stone.author: Tony Robbins and Dean Graziosi
Naya Stone.text: All this stress and suffering is almost always the result of lacking strategy. I journaled on it for weeks during my first year in business. When things are disorganized and you don't have a clear gameplan, there's a lack of direction. Which creates an underlying sensation of fear and confusion, which, in turn, generates stress. 90 percent of entrepreneurship is having faith and being okchaotic in your mind. Trusting the process. . .but this is incredibly difficult to grasp in the beginning, especially when you're worried about other needs. But you're headed in the right direction.
